author: gb9t
content:
User flagged a critical architectural concern (2026-05-09): grsec historically didn't coexist with SELinux/AppArmor — distros use those LSMs by default — so 'we absorb grsec principles' could be read as 'we reject the LSM framework'.

Confirmed user's understanding: yes, grsec was hostile to LSM. GR-RBAC was an alternative MAC framework; GRKERNSEC_* feature flags overlapped with LSM enforcement; spender publicly disputed SELinux design. SELinux + grsec was effectively pick-one.

Rookery's actual position clarified and propagated into the design corpus:

**Two-layer split for grsec absorption (now documented in references/grsec-pax-notes.md, 00-overview.md, and security/00-overview.md):**

LAYER 1 — Memory protection + CFI (LSM-orthogonal): ABSORBED
- KERNEXEC, UDEREF, PAGEEXEC, NOEXEC, MPROTECT, USERCOPY, REFCOUNT, RAP, DIRECT_CALL
- RANDSTRUCT, AUTOSLAB, PRIVATE_KSTACKS, RANDKSTACK, RANDMMAP, ASLR
- LATENT_ENTROPY, MEMORY_SANITIZE, SIZE_OVERFLOW, CONSTIFY_PLUGIN
- CLOSE_KERNEL, CLOSE_USERLAND, DELAY_FREE_ONE_PAGE
- These harden the kernel below the LSM hook layer — SELinux/AppArmor see no behavioral change.

LAYER 2 — Policy enforcement (LSM territory): NOT ABSORBED AS DEFAULTS
- GR-RBAC (alternative MAC framework)
- GRKERNSEC_HIDESYM, GRKERNSEC_HIDETASK, GRKERNSEC_PROC_USER/USERGROUP
- GRKERNSEC_DMESG, GRKERNSEC_TPE, GRKERNSEC_TPE_INVERT
- GRKERNSEC_CHROOT_* policy switches, GRKERNSEC_DENYUSB, GRKERNSEC_DEVADD
- These would either replace LSM-based MAC or change userspace-visible state in ways LSM policy authors expect to control.
- Where they have defense-in-depth value, expose as sysctl knobs in 00-security-principles.md (issue #2). NEVER as defaults; NEVER as replacements for LSM enforcement.

REQ-1 of 00-overview.md mandates SELinux + AppArmor + the other in-tree LSMs work unchanged. RHEL/Fedora ship SELinux on; Debian/Ubuntu/SUSE ship AppArmor on. Breaking either breaks the project's foundational drop-in promise.

**Rookery is grsec-flavored at the memory layer and LSM-respecting at the policy layer.**

Documents updated and knowledge pages refreshed:
- references/grsec-pax-notes.md: new section 'grsec ↔ LSMs (SELinux, AppArmor, …) — coexistence model' with the two-layer table + practical rule for design-doc authoring
- 00-overview.md: Foundational Decisions table 'Hardening source' row expanded to state the two-layer split
- security/00-overview.md: new 'Critical: grsec absorption is layered — LSMs are NOT replaced' section under Hardening, with explicit notes on what 00-security-principles.md will and will not do.
